mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained below 0.25 percent eliminates yield on safe assets and reallocates capital into equities, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals persistent labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad demand strength across consumption and residential investment sectors, a joint signal that single-series metrics miss, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST fails to add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) over their linear combination in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production signals rising operating leverage and capacity utilization for manufacturing-intensive firms, boosting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 45% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading more than 2.0 index points above its trailing 12-month average accelerates near-term monetary tightening expectations, expanding equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds its trailing 12-month average by more than 2.0 index points
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL is more than 2.0 points above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms aggregate output expansion that supports top-line corporate revenue and earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth prints coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent and a subdued consumer price index creates a high real liquidity environment that reduces corporate financing costs and equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with anchored inflation
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not outperform the best single-proxy model in predicting 20-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal immediate labor market tightening that raises near-term aggregate income and consumption forecasts, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of months.
